title: Chemical Engineer (West) HSW
description:
Job Responsibilities:

  • Lead production team and manufacturing activities as Area Owner in Battery Recycling Plant pertaining to the Production planning, Process troubleshooting and Quality control and improvement.
  • Conduct performance reviews, including resolving performance issues, discipline issue, time management, and commitment of Production team.
  • Establish the scheduling of production route sheet and team performance metrics, including monitoring output and adjusting schedules to ensure production targets are achieved.
  • Accountable for productivity, quality, and training metrics of operators, and provide regular progress reports and manufacturing metrics to management and cross-functional stakeholders, including developing resolutions as needed to improve metrics achievement.
  • Lead Production CIP (continuous improvement program), partner with engineering on investigations and/or corrective actions, perform non-conformance investigations for review with engineering, assures documentation content is valid.
  • Accountable to ensure staff is appropriately trained to perform assigned work according to production WI. (Assignment during working hour and OT hour).
  • Monitor raw material usage and availability required in daily production, including proposing resolutions to mediate shortages.
  • Manage material and labour variances to meet established standards, including proposing resolutions to mediate shortfalls.
  • Responsible for production machinery basic maintenance, maximise production output, machinery up time and monitor equipment performance with data recordings and production check list/route sheet/material transfer form/preventive maintenance records.
  • Responsible for Production floor cleanliness and safety, perform 5S and housekeeping check in daily basis.
  • Working with supervisors to improve process efficiency and stability.
  • Co-ordinate with maintenance team to drive TPM (Total Productive Maintenance) system.
  • Update monthly KPIs and present to management.
  • Update skills matrix and machine utilisation for capacity planning.

Job Requirements:

  • Degree in Chemical Engineering or other relevant Engineering disciplines.
  • Relevant manufacturing experience in chemical or process plant industries, including responsibility for production operations and process execution.
  • Demonstrates strong teamwork, collaboration, and willingness to learn.
  • Strong experience in production and process operations, including manpower planning, machine utilisation, and capacity planning.
  • Ability to perform material planning and process troubleshooting by leading supervisors and operators.


HOW TO APPLY:

Interested applicants, kindly send your resume in MS WORD format or please click on “Apply Now”.

We regret only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Important Note: Trust Recruit Pte Ltd is committed to safeguarding your personal data in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA).

Please read our privacy statement on our corporate website

www.trustrecruit.com.sg.

postedCompany.description:
Trust Recruit is Singapore’s leading relationship-based human resource and recruitment firm.


With more than 20 years of collective consulting experience, we provide personalized workforce solutions for our corporate clients, ranging from multinational corporations to the public sector, as well as small and medium enterprises.


At Trust Recruit, integrity and reliability permeate every level of our recruitment process.


TRUST is our currency of interactions; be it with our valued clients, talented job-seekers, or our own employees. Through our premium staffing services, we are committed to connecting and developing quality relationships between candidates and companies.
